foundation.title: LOOP
foundation.subtitle: For Loops
foundation.content: A Java 'for' loop is structured with an init, a test, and an update in a single part of the line. It's the standard for repeating tasks.
foundation.scenario: A 100-Meter Dash. Before opening the race, you initialize the distance (int i=0), check if you've finished (i < 100), and add a step (i++).

examples.title: The Standard For
examples.explanation: The basic 3-part loop used in almost every Java app.

examples.title: Enhanced For
examples.explanation: A simplified way to go through every item in a collection or list.

examples.title: Reverse Count
examples.explanation: Starting at 10 and counting down precisely to zero.

foundation.title: WHILE
foundation.subtitle: Until Loops
foundation.content: A 'while' loop in Java repeats as long as its condition remains True. It is evaluated at the top of every repeat.
foundation.scenario: A Waiter standing by a customer. While (the customer is still eating), the waiter stands there. He can't leave until the bowl is empty (False).

examples.title: Top-Down Test
examples.explanation: Checking the switch before ever starting the first task.

examples.title: Sentinel Loop
examples.explanation: Waiting for a user to type a specific word like 'QUIT'.

examples.title: Do-While
examples.explanation: A special loop that ALWAYS runs at least once, then checks the rule.

foundation.keyPoint: while loop checks BEFORE running. do-while checks AFTER.

foundation.title: MATH
foundation.subtitle: Math Power
foundation.content: Java handles arithmetic with high precision. It also provides the java.lang.Math toolkit for complex things like Square Roots.
foundation.scenario: An Architect's Calculator. Finding the total load (+), subtracting waste (-), multiplying materials (*), and dividing space between rooms (/).

examples.title: Basic Calc
examples.explanation: Standard math (+, -, *, /) with fixed-type variables (int or double).

examples.title: Exponentiation
examples.explanation: Using the built-in Math library to calculate powers or square roots.

examples.title: The Modulo %
examples.explanation: Finding the remainder. Helpful for switching between two players (id % 2).

foundation.keyPoint: Math.pow(base, exp) for powers. % for remainder.

gameData.q: Which Java loop runs at least once?
